reviews.author: Dmitry Ilvokhin
reviews.summary: Added the tracepoint to various slowpath unlock paths, including mutexes and rtmutexes. The tracepoint fires when there are blocked waiters being woken.

2026-03-04.patch_summary: This patch series adds a new tracepoint called contended_release to track when a lock is released due to contention. The tracepoint is added to the slowpath unlock paths of various sleepable locks, including mutexes, rtmutexes, semaphores, and rwsems.

reviews.author: Masami (Google)
reviews.summary: OK, but I don't recommend you to split the traceevent definition and its usage. Could you combine [1/3] and [3/3], so that we can understand why this event is introduced and where it is recorded by checking one commit?
reviews.raw_body:
On Wed,  4 Mar 2026 16:56:15 +0000
Dmitry Ilvokhin <d@ilvokhin.com> wrote:

> Add the contended_release trace event. This tracepoint fires on the
> holder side when a contended lock is released, complementing the
> existing contention_begin/contention_end tracepoints which fire on the
> waiter side.
> 
> This enables correlating lock hold time under contention with waiter
> events by lock address. Subsequent patches wire this tracepoint into
> the individual lock implementations.
> 

OK, but I don't recommend you to split the traceevent definition
and its usage. Could you combine [1/3] and [3/3], so that we can
understand why this event is introduced and where it is recorded
by checking one commit?

Thank you,

> Signed-off-by: Dmitry Ilvokhin <d@ilvokhin.com>
> ---
>  include/trace/events/lock.h | 17 +++++++++++++++++
>  1 file changed, 17 insertions(+)
> 
> diff --git a/include/trace/events/lock.h b/include/trace/events/lock.h
> index 8e89baa3775f..4f28e41977ec 100644
> --- a/include/trace/events/lock.h
> +++ b/include/trace/events/lock.h
> @@ -138,6 +138,23 @@ TRACE_EVENT(contention_end,
>  	TP_printk("%p (ret=%d)", __entry->lock_addr, __entry->ret)
>  );
>  
> +TRACE_EVENT(contended_release,
> +
> +	TP_PROTO(void *lock),
> +
> +	TP_ARGS(lock),
> +
> +	TP_STRUCT__entry(
> +		__field(void *, lock_addr)
> +	),
> +
> +	TP_fast_assign(
> +		__entry->lock_addr = lock;
> +	),
> +
> +	TP_printk("%p", __entry->lock_addr)
> +);
> +
>  #endif /* _TRACE_LOCK_H */
>  
>  /* This part must be outside protection */
> -- 
> 2.47.3
> 


-- 
Masami Hiramatsu (Google) <mhiramat@kernel.org>

2026-03-05.patch_summary:
Add the contended_release trace event. This tracepoint fires on the holder side when a contended lock is released, complementing the existing contention_begin/contention_end tracepoints which fire on the waiter side.

This enables correlating lock hold time under contention with waiter events by lock address. Subsequent patches wire this tracepoint into the individual lock implementations.
